title: Pressure, Temperature, and Flow Calculator Tool

requirements.text: Create a calculator app UI that lets a user calculate (1) Pressure, (2) Temperature conversion, and (3) Flow rate from user inputs, and shows the computed results immediately on the page.

quotes: mojy aik app bna dein jis me pressure level temp aur flow culclation ho

acceptanceCriteria: The home screen clearly presents three calculator sections (or tabs): Pressure, Temperature, Flow.
Each section contains labeled input fields and a prominent calculated result area.
All user-facing labels, buttons, and messages are in English.

requirements.text: Implement the following calculations with unit support and clear formulas displayed in the UI: (A) Pressure: P = F / A with inputs Force and Area and selectable units (Force: N, kN; Area: m², cm²) and output in Pa/kPa. (B) Temperature: conversions between °C, °F, and K. (C) Flow rate: Q = V / t with inputs Volume and Time and selectable units (Volume: m³, L; Time: s, min) and output in m³/s and L/s.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressure calculator computes correctly for multiple unit combinations (including proper cm²↔m² and N↔kN conversions).
Temperature converter correctly converts between all pairs of (°C, °F, K) and prevents invalid Kelvin (< 0 K).
Flow calculator computes correctly for multiple unit combinations (including L↔m³ and min↔s conversions).
Each calculator section displays the formula being used in readable form.

requirements.text: Add input validation and helpful error states for all calculators (e.g., missing inputs, non-numeric values, zero/negative area or time where not allowed) without crashing the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid inputs show an English inline error message near the relevant field(s).
When inputs are invalid, the result area does not show misleading numbers (e.g., shows “—” or “Fix inputs to calculate”).
The app remains responsive and does not throw runtime errors during typing.

requirements.text: Define and apply a coherent visual theme for the app (industrial instrument panel style) using Tailwind styling: neutral dark/gray surfaces, high-contrast typography, and a single accent color (not blue/purple) used consistently for primary actions and highlights.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI has a consistent color palette, spacing, and typography across all calculator sections.
Primary interactive elements (e.g., reset buttons, active tab) use one consistent accent color that is not blue or purple.
Layout works well on mobile and desktop widths.

constraints: Use React + TypeScript + Tailwind + React Query (no additional frontend frameworks).
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui or any other immutable paths specified by the platform.
nonGoals: Advanced fluid dynamics (e.g., Bernoulli, pipe friction) unless explicitly requested later.
